gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The RFC-0005 policy sites (§4: swap-target, packing; RFC-0008 RT3 adds placement as the"] # [doc = " third). A [`PolicySlot`] is keyed by site so a set/select is always attributed to the site"] # [doc = " it governs (provenance, RFC-0001 §4.3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum PolicySite { # [doc = " The RFC-0002 swap-target site."] SwapTarget , # [doc = " The RFC-0004 §5 packing site."] Packing , # [doc = " The RFC-0008 RT3 placement site (single-node in Phase I — DN-78 §3; the multi-node"] # [doc = " candidate set is deferred, see [`crate::r2_residual`])."] Placement , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A reified policy-set transition record (G2: a mechanized set is never a silent override —"] # [doc = " the transition itself is inspectable). Guarantee: **`Exact`** — every [`PolicySlot::set`]"] # [doc = " appends exactly one record, with a per-slot monotonic sequence number, by construction."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct PolicySetRecord { # [doc = " The site whose active policy changed."] pub site : PolicySite , # [doc = " Per-slot monotonic sequence number (0 for the first set)."] pub seq : u64 , # [doc = " The previous active policy's content address, `None` on the first set."] pub previous : Option < ContentHash > , # [doc = " The new active policy's content address ([`SelectionPolicy::policy_ref`])."] pub new_policy : ContentHash , # [doc = " The new policy's display name (for the EXPLAIN/teaching surface)."] pub new_policy_name : String , }
gaps.reason: struct `PolicySetRecord` has a field type with no confirmed mapping (`String`/`str` — `Bytes` exists in base_type but is not confirmed equivalent to a UTF-8 text type)

Guarantee: **`Exact`**"] # [doc = " — fail-closed by construction; there is no built-in fallback policy (a silent default"] # [doc = " would be a black box, ADR-006)."] NoActivePolicy { # [doc = " The site that has no active policy."] site : PolicySite , } , # [doc = " The underlying RFC-0005 selection refused (e.g. an out-of-range override)."] Select (SelectError) , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A runtime slot binding the **active** [`SelectionPolicy`] for one RFC-0005 site, with an"] # [doc = " append-only transition log and a selection trace (DN-78 §3 B-2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The slot is the mechanized *setter surface*: `set` swaps the active policy and records the"] # [doc = " transition; `select` decides through the active policy and records the mandatory"] # [doc = " [`Explanation`]. Both logs are read-only views (`transitions`/`trace`) — append-only by"] # [doc = " construction (no public mutation besides the appending operations)."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct PolicySlot { site : PolicySite , active : Option < SelectionPolicy > , transitions : Vec < PolicySetRecord > , trace : Vec < Explanation > , }
gaps.reason: struct `PolicySlot` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl PolicySlot { # [doc = " An empty slot for `site` — no active policy, no transitions, no trace."] # [must_use] pub fn new (site : PolicySite) -> Self { PolicySlot { site , active : None , transitions : Vec :: new () , trace : Vec :: new () , } } # [doc = " The site this slot governs."] # [must_use] pub fn site (& self) -> PolicySite { self . site } # [doc = " Set the active policy, appending a [`PolicySetRecord`] (returned by reference)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: **`Exact`** — exactly one record is appended per call, `seq` is the per-slot"] # [doc = " monotonic count, and `previous` is the outgoing policy's content address (`None` on the"] # [doc = " first set). The transition is never silent (G2)."] pub fn set (& mut self , policy : SelectionPolicy) -> & PolicySetRecord { let record = PolicySetRecord { site : self . site , seq : self . transitions . len () as u64 , previous : self . active . as_ref () . map (SelectionPolicy :: policy_ref) , new_policy : policy . policy_ref () , new_policy_name : policy . name () . to_owned () , } ; self . active = Some (policy) ; self . transitions . push (record) ; self . transitions . last () . expect ("push in the line above guarantees a last element") } # [doc = " The active policy, if one has been set. `None` is not a fallback state — selection"] # [doc = " through an unset slot refuses explicitly ([`SlotError::NoActivePolicy`])."] # [must_use] pub fn active (& self) -> Option < & SelectionPolicy > { self . active . as_ref () } # [doc = " The append-only transition log (every `set`, in order)."] # [must_use] pub fn transitions (& self) -> & [PolicySetRecord] { & self . transitions } # [doc = " The selection trace: the mandatory [`Explanation`] of every selection made through this"] # [doc = " slot, in order — extractable for capture/diffing (DN-78 §3 B-2)."] # [must_use] pub fn trace (& self) -> & [Explanation] { & self . trace } # [doc = " Decide through the active policy (RFC-0005 `select`), recording the mandatory"] # [doc = " [`Explanation`] into the slot's trace."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Errors are explicit: an unset slot is [`SlotError::NoActivePolicy`] (never a silent"] # [doc = " default — G2/ADR-006); an underlying selection refusal passes through as"] # [doc = " [`SlotError::Select`]."] pub fn select (& mut self , inputs : & SelectionInputs , forced : Option < usize > ,) -> Result < (Candidate , Explanation) , SlotError > { let site = self . site ; let policy = self . active . as_ref () . ok_or (SlotError :: NoActivePolicy { site }) ? ; let (candidate , explanation) = select (policy , inputs , forced) ? ; self . trace . push (explanation . clone ()) ; Ok ((candidate , explanation)) } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Why a capture failed — always explicit (G2), never a silent reconstruction."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum CaptureError { # [doc = " The recorded `policy_ref` resolves to nothing in the given registry. Guarantee:"] # [doc = " **`Exact`** — fail-closed by construction; capture never fabricates a policy."] UnknownPolicyRef { # [doc = " The unresolvable content address."] policy_ref : ContentHash , } , # [doc = " The registry returned a policy whose own content address differs from the requested one"] # [doc = " (a corrupted registry). Never silently accepted."] RefMismatch { # [doc = " The content address the capture asked for."] requested : ContentHash , # [doc = " The content address the resolved policy actually hashes to."] resolved : ContentHash , } , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Materialize the policy that decided `explanation` from `registry` (DN-78 §3 B-1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: **`Exact`** for the resolution contract — an unknown ref is an explicit"] # [doc = " [`CaptureError::UnknownPolicyRef`] and a hash mismatch an explicit"] # [doc = " [`CaptureError::RefMismatch`]; a returned [`CapturedPolicy`] always satisfies"] # [doc = " `policy.policy_ref() == policy_ref` (checked here, not assumed)."] pub fn capture (registry : & PolicyRegistry , explanation : & Explanation ,) -> Result < CapturedPolicy , CaptureError > { let requested = explanation . policy . clone () ; let policy = registry . get (& requested) . ok_or_else (| | CaptureError :: UnknownPolicyRef { policy_ref : requested . clone () , }) ? ; let resolved = policy . policy_ref () ; if resolved != requested { return Err (CaptureError :: RefMismatch { requested , resolved , }) ; } Ok (CapturedPolicy { policy_ref : requested , policy : policy . clone () , }) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Why a replay failed — always explicit (G2), never a silent pass."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub enum ReplayError { # [doc = " The captured policy is not the one the record claims decided it (`policy_ref`"] # [doc = " mismatch) — replaying against the wrong policy would be a silent apples-to-oranges"] # [doc = " comparison, so it refuses up front."] PolicyMismatch { # [doc = " The record's policy content address."] recorded : ContentHash , # [doc = " The captured policy's content address."] captured : ContentHash , } , # [doc = " Re-running the recorded inputs refused (e.g. a recorded override index that no longer"] # [doc = " fits the policy — impossible for a faithful capture, but never silently swallowed)."] Select (SelectError) , # [doc = " The replayed decision differs from the recorded one. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Replay a recorded decision against its captured policy (DN-78 §3 B-1): re-run the recorded"] # [doc = " inputs — honoring the recorded override state — and require the identical [`Explanation`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: **`Empirical`** for \"replay reaches the recorded decision\" — the record-vs-replay"] # [doc = " differential is property-tested over randomized policies/inputs; RFC-0005 `select` is"] # [doc = " deterministic (same `(policy, inputs, forced)` → same result) but that determinism carries"] # [doc = " no mechanized theorem, so the claim is not `Proven` (VR-5; M-964 audit). A divergence is an"] # [doc = " explicit [`ReplayError::Diverged`] carrying both records for inspection (G2)."] pub fn replay (captured : & CapturedPolicy , recorded : & Explanation ,) -> Result < Explanation , ReplayError > { if captured . policy_ref != recorded . policy { return Err (ReplayError :: PolicyMismatch { recorded : recorded . policy . clone () , captured : captured . policy_ref . clone () , }) ; } let forced = recorded . overridden . then_some (recorded . chosen_index) ; let (_ , replayed) = select (& captured . policy , & recorded . inputs , forced) ? ; if replayed != * recorded { return Err (ReplayError :: Diverged { recorded : Box :: new (recorded . clone ()) , replayed : Box :: new (replayed) , }) ; } Ok (replayed) }
